resume:
Junior Accountant — Accounts Payable & Month-End Close

Fairmont Logistics — Junior Accountant (2 years)
- Process ~600 vendor invoices/month; own the 3-way match exceptions queue
- Prepare AP accruals and reconcile the AP subledger to the GL at month-end
- Reduced unmatched-invoice backlog as part of a process cleanup

Greenfield Retail — AP Clerk (1 year)
- Entered invoices and processed weekly check runs

Education: B.S. Accounting. CPA candidate (passed FAR, REG).
Skills: NetSuite, Excel (XLOOKUP, pivot tables), 3-way matching, GL reconciliation.
jd: Fairmont Logistics is hiring a Junior Accountant for the AP and close team. You will own the 3-way match exception queue, prepare AP accruals, and reconcile the AP subledger to the GL each month. We expect tight, specific answers: how you cleared a backlog, what a reconciliation difference turned out to be, how you decide an invoice is safe to release. Required: 1-3 years AP experience, NetSuite or comparable ERP, and a precise grasp of accruals and the 3-way match. The loop includes a reconciliation exercise where you explain your work step by step.

history.q: Describe your current AP responsibilities at Fairmont.
Walk me through how the 3-way match works in your process.
What's the most common reason an invoice lands in your exception queue?
How do you decide an exception is safe to release for payment?
Tell me about the unmatched-invoice backlog cleanup you mentioned.
How do you prepare the AP accrual at month-end?
Walk me through reconciling the AP subledger to the GL.
Tell me about a reconciliation difference you had to chase down.
What did that difference turn out to be?
How do you avoid duplicate payments?
On that backlog cleanup — give me the starting number, what you changed, and where it ended up.

candidate_last_answer: When I started, the unmatched queue was about 340 invoices, some over 60 days old. I sorted them by root cause and found roughly 70% were price mismatches under five dollars caused by freight rounding. I proposed a tolerance threshold of five dollars in NetSuite for that vendor group, which auto-cleared those. The rest I worked manually, oldest first, about 30 a day. Over six weeks the queue dropped to under 50, and we held it there with a weekly review.

top_question_traits: must-anchor-on: I proposed a tolerance threshold of five dollars in NetSuite for that vendor group

expected-yield: who approved the five-dollar tolerance and what control risk it introduced — whether the candidate considered that auto-clearing sub-five-dollar mismatches could mask a systematic pricing error, and what compensating check they added
